title: Getting Started with RCausalML
text: RCausalML R port Python CausalML EconML packages. provides unified interface estimating Conditional Average Treatment Effect (CATE) experimental observational data, covering:

title: Getting Started with RCausalML
text: Core R dependencies installed automatically. Neural models additionally require torch package:

title: Getting Started with RCausalML
text: T-Learner fits separate outcome models treated control units estimates CATE difference.
code: library(RCausalML)  # Simulate a simple randomised trial set.seed(42) n   <- 500 X   <- matrix(rnorm(n * 5), n, 5) W   <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.5) tau <- 2 + X[, 1]            # heterogeneous treatment effect Y   <- tau * W + rnorm(n)  # Fit the T-Learner tl  <- TLearner$new(learner = "ranger") tl$fit(X, W, Y)  # Predict CATE on new data X_test <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 5), 100, 5) cate   <- tl$predict(X_test) head(cate)

title: Getting Started with RCausalML
text: Double Machine Learning (DML) removes confounding bias nuisance models via cross-fitting estimating treatment effect.
code: library(RCausalML)  set.seed(42) n    <- 1000 X    <- matrix(rnorm(n * 10), n, 10) W    <- as.numeric(X[, 1] > 0) + rnorm(n, sd = 0.1) Y    <- 2 * W + X[, 1] + rnorm(n)  dml  <- LinearDML$new(   model_y      = "ranger",   model_t      = "ranger",   n_folds      = 5 ) dml$fit(X, W, Y)  ate  <- dml$effect() cat("Estimated ATE:", round(ate, 3), "\n")

title: Getting Started with RCausalML
text: Uplift forests directly optimise difference outcomes treated control units split.
code: library(RCausalML)  # Simulate uplift dataset df <- make_uplift_classification(   n_samples  = 2000,   treatment_name = "treatment" )  # KL-based uplift random forest uf <- uplift_rf_kl(   formula   = ~ . - treatment - outcome,   data      = df,   treatment = df$treatment,   outcome   = df$outcome )  # Gain and Qini curves gain_df <- gain_curve(uf, df$treatment, df$outcome) plot_gain(gain_df)

title: Getting Started with RCausalML
code: library(RCausalML)  set.seed(42) n  <- 800 X  <- matrix(rnorm(n * 10), n, 10) W  <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.4) Y  <- (1 + X[, 1]) * W + rnorm(n)  cf  <- grf_causal_forest(X, Y, W)  # Average treatment effect grf_average_treatment_effect(cf)  # Variable importance grf_variable_importance(cf)

title: Getting Started with RCausalML
text: DragonNet jointly estimates propensity score potential outcomes using shared representation targeted regularisation term.
code: library(RCausalML)  set.seed(42) n   <- 600 X   <- matrix(rnorm(n * 8), n, 8) W   <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(X[, 1])) Y   <- (0.5 + X[, 2]) * W + rnorm(n)  net <- dragonnet(X, W, Y,   hidden_size = 64,   epochs      = 100,   batch_size  = 64 )  preds <- predict(net, X) head(preds)

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: RCausalML R package uplift modeling causal inference, inspired Python’s CausalML Microsoft’s EconML. features automated ML nuisance models, policy learning, CATE interpretation, DR-learner family, multiple neural causal models (CEVAE, DragonNet, GANITE, ) support SCMs, DAG learning (NOTEARS, DAG-GNN, GraN-DAG), GNN transformer-based causal models, counterfactual analysis. Includes tools causal structure learning example notebooks. neural modules require R torch package. Causal inference machine learning closely linked areas data science. Causal inference aims identify cause--effect relationships, machine learning seeks patterns makes predictions, often based associations rather direct causes. uncover real causal effects, causal inference uses methods like randomized controlled trials, instrumental variables, propensity score matching, especially running experiments possible ethical. Machine learning strong working large complex datasets tasks like classification, prediction, pattern recognition, automatically tell us causes . Lately, two fields come together “causal machine learning,” brings best . Machine learning can help causal inference automatically adjusting confounders estimating treatment effects complex data. time, causal inference can make machine learning models easier understand reliable. combination especially useful areas healthcare, economics, technology, important understand might happen . working together, methods help create AI systems predict outcomes also explain reasons behind .

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: package estimates Conditional Average Treatment Effect (CATE) related quantities experimental observational data using meta-learners, propensity scoring, matching, uplift trees, policy learning, instrumental variables.

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: Three core modules align EconML’s API concepts: R/DMLearner.R — Double Machine Learning (DML): Chernozhukov et al.–style DML cross-fitting; outcome treatment fitted flexible ML (ranger, glmnet, xgb, lm), final stage (linear, sparse linear, kernel, nonparametric, causal forest) estimates CATE residuals. Includes LinearDML, SparseLinearDML, KernelDML, NonParamDML, CausalForestDML, plus native R DynamicDMLearner (panel/sequential treatments) OrthoIVLearner / DMLIVLearner / NonParamDMLIVLearner DML instrumental variables. predict.DMLearner robust near-degenerate OLS final stages (aliased lm coefficients replaced zero) guarantees finite output callers including explain_cate() / kernelshap. DoubleML, mlr3, mlr3learners, mlr3measures installed, -style DML Δy=y1−y0\Delta y = y_1 - y_0 available via doubleml_did_linear, doubleml_did_rf, doubleml_did_xgboost, optional nuisance metrics doubleml_did_eval_* doubleml_did_eval_preds; doubleml_plr, doubleml_plr_fit_data (pre-built DoubleMLData + mlr3 learners, including pipelines / ensembles), doubleml_plr_tune_data (mlr3tuning), doubleml_pliv fit partially linear regression PLIV DoubleML R package; doubleml_data_from_data_frame / doubleml_data_from_matrix build DoubleMLData; doubleml_make_plr_CCDDHNR2018, doubleml_make_pliv_CHS2015, doubleml_fetch_401k, doubleml_fetch_bonus provide example / simulated data aligned DoubleML. R/cate_interpreter.R — CATE interpreters: Single-tree interpretation CATE policy model. SingleTreeCateInterpreter fits regression tree predicted CATE summarize effect heterogeneity covariates; SingleTreePolicyInterpreter fits classification tree predicts optimal treatment (treat) covariates. API matches EconML’s econml.cate_interpreter. R/policy_learner.R — Policy learning: Learns treatment assignment policy maximize expected outcome (welfare). policy_learner implements Athey & Wager (2018) doubly robust weighted classification; DRPolicyTree DRPolicyForest provide cross-fitted outcome + policy tree/forest (EconML-style). Supports fit, predict, predict_proba, (tree/forest) feature_importances predict_value. R/automated_ml.R — Automated ML: Automate model selection nuisance models (Y, T) causal estimators. EconAutoMLConfig configures constraints (linear-, sample-weights required) task (regression/classification). AutomatedMLModel selects learner via cross-validation whitelist (lm, glmnet, ranger, rpart), fits exposes fit, predict, predict_proba. Use add_automated_ml pass configs meta-learners. Local AutoML (Azure); API aligned EconML’s econml.automated_ml.

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: Run package root (directory contains DESCRIPTION). install missing R package dependencies declared DESCRIPTION (Imports/Depends/Suggests), install RCausalML.
code: install_missing <- function(pkgs, repos = getOption("repos")) {   pkgs <- unique(pkgs)   pkgs <- pkgs[nzchar(pkgs)]   pkgs <- setdiff(pkgs, rownames(installed.packages()))   if (length(pkgs)) install.packages(pkgs, repos = repos)   invisible(TRUE) }  desc <- read.dcf("DESCRIPTION")[1, , drop = FALSE] fields <- intersect(c("Depends", "Imports", "Suggests"), colnames(desc)) deps_raw <- paste(desc[1, fields], collapse = ",")  # remove version constraints like "(>= 1.2)" and split deps_raw <- gsub("\\s*\\([^\\)]+\\)", "", deps_raw) deps <- trimws(unlist(strsplit(deps_raw, ","))) deps <- setdiff(deps, c("R"))  install_missing(c("remotes"))  # used below (lightweight) install_missing(deps)  remotes::install_local(".", dependencies = FALSE, upgrade = "never")

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: Build archive package root (writes RCausalML_0.3.0.tar.gz next DESCRIPTION): Ubuntu: Windows (PowerShell / R console): Package name: RCausalML (version 0.2.0). Load library(RCausalML). Dependencies: glmnet, ranger, rpart, R6, expm, igraph, htetree. Optional: grf, MatchIt, xgboost, reticulate, devtools (load_all standalone test scripts). DoubleML helpers R/DMLearner.R (PLR, PLIV, , DoubleMLData wrappers, simulators / fetch): install DoubleML, mlr3, mlr3learners, mlr3measures. NOTEARS (R/causalDeepNet.R): expm, igraph; torch nonlinear NOTEARS (MLP/Sobolev). DAG-GNN GraN-DAG (R/causalDeepNet.R): torch, R6, expm; ranger progress optional GraN-DAG PNS progress bars. causalStructureML (R/causalStructureML.R): NOTEARS / DAG-GNN / GraN-DAG depending method. SHAP / shapviz (see ): kernelshap, shapviz. causalDeepNet (R/causalDeepNet.R): torch (>= 0.10) (CEVAE, DragonNet, TARNet, CFRNet, GANITE, CausalGAN, DSCM, CausalDiscrepancyVAE, neural Granger models cMLP, cLSTM, EconomySRU, NRI via neural_granger_ml(), SCM Deep Components: deep_scm, deci_model, dynotears); nnet / ranger placeholders applicable. SCM graph utilities (plot_scm_dag): reshape2 (optional, falls back base graphics). tabular DCEVAE (optional, exported build): R package reticulate Python environment torch, numpy, tqdm (code inst/dcevae/DCEVAE_ours/).

Install optional packages: install.packages(c("kernelshap", "shapviz")). Use explain_cate(model, X, ...) RCausalML get kernelshap object fitted model feature matrix X. Pass object shapviz() build shapviz object. Use shapviz function: sv_importance(), sv_dependence(), sv_waterfall(), sv_force(), sv_dependence2D(), etc. Works meta-learners (S/T/X/R/DR), DML (Linear, SparseLinear, Kernel, NonParam, CausalForest, Dynamic, IV), causal forest, uplift trees/forests, policy learners, CATE interpreters, neural models (CEVAE, DragonNet, TARNet, CFRNet, GANITE, CausalGAN, DSCM, CausalDiscrepancyVAE, neural Granger models neural_granger_ml(), DeepSCM / DECI / DynoTEARS via deep_scm() / deci_model() / dynotears()), fitters predict(object, newdata). optional tabular DCEVAE bridge (inst/) uses specialized predict(fit, r, d, , y) signature rather newdata alone code path wired .

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: RCausalML integrates causaldata R package (Huntington-Klein & Barrett), provides example datasets causal inference textbooks (Effect, Causal Inference: Mixtape, Causal Inference: ). Use load_causaldata(name) load dataset RCausalML-ready form (covariates X, treatment w, outcome y, optionally instrument Z), list_causaldata_datasets() see supported names variable roles. Install data package: install.packages("causaldata"). publishing reporting results use datasets, cite causaldata original source given citation component list returned load_causaldata(). See ?explain_cate arguments (n_samples, bg_X, use_permshap, options passed kernelshap/permshap).
code: library(RCausalML) list_causaldata_datasets()   # nsw_mixtape, cps_mixtape, abortion, close_college, ... d <- load_causaldata("nsw_mixtape") sl <- SLearner(learner = "ranger") sl <- fit(sl, d$X, d$w, d$y) estimate_ate(sl, d$X, d$w, d$y) # Always cite the data source (returned in d$citation) and the causaldata package: citation("causaldata") library(RCausalML) library(kernelshap) library(shapviz)  d <- synthetic_data(mode = 1, n = 500, p = 5, sigma = 1) sl <- SLearner(learner = "ranger") sl <- fit(sl, d$X, d$w, d$y)  # SHAP for CATE; then all shapviz plots ks <- explain_cate(sl, d$X[1:100, ], bg_X = d$X, use_permshap = TRUE) shp <- shapviz(ks) sv_importance(shp) sv_dependence(shp, "X1") sv_waterfall(shp, 1) sv_force(shp, 1)

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: run faster multi-core machines (Unix/macOS), use n_cores argument supported: Meta-learners (fit_predict, estimate_ate return_ci=TRUE bootstrap_ci=TRUE): bootstrap runs parallel, e.g. fit_predict(sl, X, w, y, return_ci = TRUE, n_cores = 4) estimate_ate(sl, X, w, y, bootstrap_ci = TRUE, n_cores = 4). DR-Learner: n_cores bootstrap fit_predict.DRLearner estimate_ate.DRLearner. Uplift forests (uplift_rf_kl, uplift_rf_ed, uplift_rf_chi, uplift_rf_cts, uplift_randomForest): tree building runs parallel, e.g. uplift_randomForest(X, w, y, n_estimators = 100, n_cores = 4). Parallelization uses parallel::mclapply (multi-core lapply). Windows, n_cores > 1 ignored runs remain sequential.

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: Run quickstart example (see inst/examples/quickstart.R): Example output:
code: library(RCausalML)  # Synthetic data (Nie & Wager 2018 style) set.seed(42) d <- synthetic_data(mode = 1, n = 1000, p = 5, sigma = 1) y <- d$y X <- d$X w <- d$w          # treatment (0/1) e <- d$e          # propensity (for X-Learner, R-Learner, TMLE)  # S-Learner (LRSRegressor = SLearner(learner = "lm")) lr <- LRSRegressor() lr <- fit(lr, X, w, y) ate_lr <- estimate_ate(lr, X, w, y, return_ci = TRUE)  # S-Learner with ranger sl <- SLearner(learner = "ranger") sl <- fit(sl, X, w, y) ate_sl <- estimate_ate(sl, X, w, y, return_ci = TRUE)  # T-Learner tl <- TLearner(learner = "ranger") tl <- fit(tl, X, w, y) ate_tl <- estimate_ate(tl, X, w, y, return_ci = TRUE)  # X-Learner (with propensity) xl <- XLearner(learner = "ranger") xl <- fit(xl, X, w, y, p = e) ate_xl <- estimate_ate(xl, X, w, y, p = e, return_ci = TRUE)  # R-Learner rl <- RLearner(learner = "ranger", n_fold = 5) rl <- fit(rl, X, w, y, p = e) ate_rl <- estimate_ate(rl, X, w, y, p = e, return_ci = TRUE)  # TMLE tmle <- TMLELearner(learner = "ranger") tmle <- fit(tmle, X, w, y, p = e) ate_tmle <- estimate_ate(tmle, X, w, y, p = e, return_ci = TRUE)  # Propensity and CATE ps <- propensity_glmnet(X, w, n_fold = 5) cate_s <- predict(lr, X, verbose = FALSE) cate_t <- predict(tl, X) ATE (S-Learner / LRSRegressor): 0.60 (0.46, 0.75) ATE (S-Learner ranger): 0.48 (0.41, 0.55) ATE (T-Learner ranger): 0.56 (0.49, 0.64) ATE (X-Learner ranger): 0.49 (0.42, 0.55) ATE (R-Learner ranger): 0.52 (0.37, 0.68) ATE (TMLE ranger): 0.44 (0.34, 0.54) Propensity scores (first 5): 0.8517243 0.5155485 0.6182359 0.7123617 0.6122691 Mean CATE (S-Learner): 0.6048919   Mean CATE (T-Learner): 0.5633381

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
code: # Unified uplift random forest (auto-detects classification vs regression) uf <- uplift_randomForest(X, w, y, n_estimators = 100, evaluation_function = "KL") cate_u <- predict(uf, X)  # Or explicitly for binary / continuous outcomes: # uplift_randomForestClassifier(X, w, y, n_estimators = 100) # uplift_randomForestRegressor(X, w, y, n_estimators = 100, evaluation_function = "TLearner")  # Legacy entry points (same underlying tree builders): # uf <- uplift_rf_kl(X, w, y, n_trees = 100) # cate_u <- predict(uf, X)  # 2SLS with instrument Z # iv_fit <- iv_2sls(Y, W, Z, X) # LATE: late_iv(Y, W, Z)

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
code: # Policy learner (Athey & Wager: who to treat) — doubly robust + classifier d <- synthetic_data(mode = 1, n = 800, p = 6, sigma = 1) pl <- policy_learner(outcome_learner = "ranger", treatment_learner = "glmnet", policy_learner = "rpart") pl <- fit(pl, d$X, d$w, d$y, control = rpart::rpart.control(maxdepth = 2)) policy_pred <- predict(pl, d$X)   # 0 = control, 1 = treat prob_treat <- predict_proba(pl, d$X)  # DR Policy Tree (EconML-style): cross-fitted outcome + single policy tree pt <- DRPolicyTree(model_regression = "ranger", cv = 2L, random_state = 42) pt <- fit(pt, d$X, d$w, d$y) pred_pt <- predict(pt, d$X) proba_pt <- predict_proba(pt, d$X) imp_pt <- feature_importances(pt)  # DR Policy Forest: ensemble of policy trees (majority vote) pf <- DRPolicyForest(n_estimators = 50L, cv = 2L, max_samples = 0.6, random_state = 123) pf <- fit(pf, d$X, d$w, d$y) pred_pf <- predict(pf, d$X)  # CATE interpreter: single tree over CATE (interpret heterogeneity) sl <- SLearner(learner = "ranger"); sl <- fit(sl, d$X, d$w, d$y) interp_cate <- SingleTreeCateInterpreter(max_depth = 3L, min_samples_leaf = 10L) interp_cate <- interpret(interp_cate, sl, d$X) cate_tree <- predict(interp_cate, d$X)   # tree-predicted CATE  # Policy interpreter: single tree over optimal treatment (who to treat) pol_interp <- SingleTreePolicyInterpreter(max_depth = 3L, min_samples_leaf = 10L) pol_interp <- interpret(pol_interp, sl, d$X) trt_rec <- treat(pol_interp, d$X)         # recommended treatment (0/1)

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: Use EconAutoMLConfig AutomatedMLModel auto-select nuisance models (e.g. outcome treatment) constraints (linear-, sample-weights). Assign result fit() fitted model stored.
code: # AutoML config: only models that support sample weights (e.g. for DR) cfg <- EconAutoMLConfig(sample_weights_required = TRUE, task = "regression", show_output = TRUE) m <- AutomatedMLModel(cfg) m <- fit(m, X, y)           # CV selects best of lm, glmnet, ranger, rpart; refit on full data pred <- predict(m, X)  # Classification (propensity): fit and predict_proba cfg_cls <- EconAutoMLConfig(task = "classification", show_output = FALSE, n_folds = 3) m_cls <- AutomatedMLModel(cfg_cls) m_cls <- fit(m_cls, X, treatment) prob <- predict_proba(m_cls, X)  # Replace configs with models in a list (e.g. for passing to meta-learners) args <- add_automated_ml(list(learner = cfg), workspace = NULL) # Counterfactual value estimator # cve <- counterfactual_value_estimator(treatment, control_name, treatment_names, #   y_proba = matrix(...), value = 1, conversion_cost = 0, impression_cost = 0) # best_idx <- predict_best_treatment(cve)

Table contents

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
code: set.seed(123) n <- 1000 p_x <- 5 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p_x), n, p_x) colnames(X) <- paste0("X", 1:p_x) W <- matrix(rnorm(n * 5), n, 5)  # Propensity: Pr[T=1 | X, W] propensity <- 1 / (1 + exp(-X[, 1] - 0.5 * W[, 1])) T_bin <- rbinom(n, 1, propensity)  # True CATE: non-linear in X1 true_cate <- 1 + 0.5 * X[, 1] + 0.3 * (X[, 1]^2) Y <- as.vector(2 + 0.3 * (W[, 1:3] %*% c(1, -0.5, 0.5)) + true_cate * T_bin + rnorm(n, 0, 0.5))  x_cols <- paste0("X", 1:p_x) data <- data.frame(   Y = Y, T = factor(T_bin), T_num = T_bin,   as.data.frame(X), W1 = W[, 1], W2 = W[, 2], W3 = W[, 3], true_cate = true_cate ) X_mat <- as.matrix(data[, x_cols]) treatment <- as.integer(data$T_num) y <- as.numeric(data$Y)

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: tests/test-uplift-trees.R runs uplift trees forests (R/uplift_trees.R) synthetic binary-outcome data make_uplift_classification(): single trees (DDP, IDDP, , CIT), uplift RF (KL, ED, CTS), multi-treatment uplift_rf_multi().
code: # Minimal example (from test): synthetic data + single tree + forest library(RCausalML) set.seed(42) out <- make_uplift_classification(   treatment_name = c("control", "treatment1"),   n_samples = 1200, n_classification_features = 8,   n_uplift_increase_dict = list(treatment1 = 3),   n_uplift_decrease_dict = list(treatment1 = 2),   delta_uplift_increase_dict = list(treatment1 = 0.12),   delta_uplift_decrease_dict = list(treatment1 = -0.08),   random_seed = 42 ) df <- out$data X <- as.matrix(df[, out$X_names]) w <- as.integer(df$treatment_group_key != "control") y <- df$conversion # Single trees tree_ddp <- uplift_tree_ddp(X, w, y, min_node_size = 30, max_depth = 4) tau_ddp <- predict(tree_ddp, X) # Uplift RF with full_output rf_kl <- uplift_rf_kl(X, w, y, n_trees = 5, min_node_size = 30, max_depth = 4, random_state = 123) pred_full <- predict(rf_kl, X, full_output = TRUE)  # control, treatment1, delta_treatment1 Rscript tests/test-uplift-trees.R

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
code: library(RCausalML) set.seed(42) n <- 300 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * 5), n, 5) colnames(X) <- paste0("X", 1:5) treatment <- rbinom(n, 1, 1 / (1 + exp(-X[, 1]))) true_cate <- 0.3 + 0.5 * X[, 1] y <- 2 + 0.3 * X[, 1] + true_cate * treatment + rnorm(n, 0, 0.5)  # LinearDML (EconML-style) dml <- LinearDML(model_y = "ranger", model_t = "ranger", n_fold = 3, seed = 123) dml <- fit(dml, X, treatment, y) te <- predict(dml, X) ate <- estimate_ate(dml, X, treatment, y, pretrain = TRUE, return_ci = TRUE)  # SparseLinearDML, KernelDML, NonParamDML, CausalForestDML (if grf installed) # dml_sparse <- SparseLinearDML(n_fold = 3); dml_sparse <- fit(dml_sparse, X, treatment, y) # dml_cf <- CausalForestDML(n_fold = 3); dml_cf <- fit(dml_cf, X, treatment, y)  # Dynamic DML (panel) and DML with IV (native R) # DynamicDMLearner, OrthoIVLearner, DMLIVLearner, NonParamDMLIVLearner # dyn <- DynamicDMLearner(cv = 2); dyn <- fit(dyn, Y, T, X, groups = unit_id) # pred_dyn <- predict(dyn, X_new); eff <- effect(dyn, newdata = X_new, T0 = 0, T1 = 1) # oiv <- OrthoIVLearner(cv = 2); oiv <- fit(oiv, Y = y, T = T, Z = Z, X = X) # cate_iv <- predict(oiv, X_new)

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: tests/test-neural.R runs neural network–based algorithms RCausalML. sets RCAUSALML_SOURCE_ROOT DCEVAE can find inst/python inst/dcevae script source()s R/causalDeepNet.R without installing package. Available models: CEVAE — Counterfactual Variational Autoencoder; generative model latent confounders (uses torch available). Ref: Louizos et al. (2017). DragonNet — Shared representation plus three heads targeted regularization (uses torch available). TARNet — Shared representation plus two heads (treatment-agnostic). CFRNet — TARNet MMD balancing. GANITE — Causal inference GANs: Generator, Discriminator, Inference net (uses torch available). test passes iterations (epochs) control training length. CausalGAN — Structural equation GAN (X,T,Y)(X, T, Y) node-wise generators interventional sampling (uses torch available). test path can run small epochs quick validation. DSCM — Deep Structural Causal Model latent abduction abduction-action-prediction counterfactual inference (uses torch available). CausalDiscrepancyVAE — Discrepancy-regularized VAE latent MMD balancing treatment-specific outcome heads (uses torch available). Neural Granger models — neural_granger_ml() wrapper cMLP, cLSTM, EconomySRU, NRI multivariate time series (uses torch available). DCEVAE — Tabular deep counterfactual equivariant VAE (Python implementation inst/dcevae/DCEVAE_ours/, called via reticulate). Skipped reticulate installed Python lacks torch / numpy / tqdm.
code: # Quick run (fewer epochs / iterations) QUICK=1 Rscript tests/test-neural.R  # Full run Rscript tests/test-neural.R

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: R/causalDeepNet.R exposes three attention-based causal models multivariate time series (v0.2.0, 03_attention_transforner_causalML.ipynb): TCDFNet (tcdf_model) — stacked causal dilated convolutions + attention head; inspired Nauta et al. (2019). CausalTransformer (causal_transformer_model) — Transformer encoder autoregressive mask + inter-variable cross-attention. TFT (tft_model) — Temporal Fusion Transformer (Lim et al., 2021): variable-selection networks, LSTM encoder, multi-head temporal attention. three also available unified wrapper attn_causal_model(). Causal graphs (variable--variable influence matrices) extracted causal_matrix_attn(). Requires torch.
code: library(RCausalML) library(torch)  set.seed(42) d <- 5L; T_len <- 300L A <- matrix(0, d, d) for (i in seq_len(d)) { A[i,i] <- 0.45; if (i > 1) A[i, i-1] <- 0.25 } x <- matrix(0, T_len, d) x[1, ] <- rnorm(d) for (t in 2:T_len) x[t, ] <- x[t-1, ] %*% t(A) + 0.15 * rnorm(d) colnames(x) <- paste0("V", seq_len(d))  # Fit all three models jointly fit <- attn_causal_model(x, lag = 10L,                          models = c("tcdf", "causal_transformer", "tft"),                          epochs = 20L, d_model = 64L, hidden = 48L, verbose = TRUE)  # Validation MSE per model print(fit$val_mse)  # Causal matrices (variable-to-variable influence) print(round(causal_matrix_attn(fit, "tcdf"), 3)) print(round(causal_matrix_attn(fit, "causal_transformer"), 3)) print(round(causal_matrix_attn(fit, "tft"), 3))  # One-step-ahead predictions ds   <- RCausalML:::.deepnet_attn_build_dataset(x, lag = 10L) preds <- predict(fit, model = "causal_transformer", newdata = ds$X[1:5, , ])  # Convenience single-model wrappers fit_tcdf <- tcdf_model(x, lag = 10L, epochs = 20L) fit_ctrf <- causal_transformer_model(x, lag = 10L, epochs = 20L, d_model = 64L) fit_tft  <- tft_model(x, lag = 10L, epochs = 20L, d_model = 64L)

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: R/causalDeepNet.R exposes three RNN/LSTM-based causal models multivariate time series (v0.2.0, 04_rnn_lstm_causalML.ipynb): CausalLSTM (causal_lstm_model) — per-variable LSTM learnable sparse causal-adjacency mask G∈[0,1]d×dG \[0,1]^{d \times d}; mask regularised L1 sparsity penalty true predictors survive. RETAIN (retain_model) — REverse Time AttentIoN (Choi et al., 2016): two-channel GRU reverse time — temporal attention α\alpha (step matters) + variable attention β\beta (variable step) — produces per-step attribution matrix interpretability. Intervention-Aware RNN (intervention_rnn_model) — GRU regime detector infers latent system states; learned regime embeddings explicit binary/continuous intervention-indicator channel concatenated input LSTM forecaster; exposes regime-conditioned causal effect matrices. three available unified wrapper rnn_causal_model(). Causal graphs extracted causal_matrix_rnn(). Requires torch.
code: library(RCausalML) library(torch)  set.seed(42) d <- 5L; T_len <- 300L A <- matrix(0, d, d) for (i in seq_len(d)) { A[i,i] <- 0.45; if (i > 1) A[i, i-1] <- 0.25 } x <- matrix(0, T_len, d) x[1, ] <- rnorm(d) for (t in 2:T_len) x[t, ] <- x[t-1, ] %*% t(A) + 0.15 * rnorm(d) colnames(x) <- paste0("V", seq_len(d))  # Intervention vector (e.g., volatility-spike proxy, top 25% quantile) interv <- as.numeric(apply(x, 1, function(r) sum(r^2)) >                      quantile(apply(x, 1, function(r) sum(r^2)), 0.75))  # Fit all three models jointly fit <- rnn_causal_model(x, lag = 10L,                         models = c("causal_lstm", "retain", "intervention_rnn"),                         intervention = interv,                         epochs = 30L, hidden = 32L, verbose = TRUE)  # Validation MSE per model print(fit$val_mse)  # Causal matrices (variable-to-variable influence) print(round(causal_matrix_rnn(fit, "causal_lstm"), 3)) print(round(causal_matrix_rnn(fit, "retain"), 3)) print(round(causal_matrix_rnn(fit, "intervention_rnn"), 3))  # One-step-ahead predictions ds    <- RCausalML:::.deepnet_rnn_build_dataset(x, lag = 10L, intervention = interv) preds <- predict(fit, model = "causal_lstm", newdata = ds$X[1:5, , ])  # Convenience single-model wrappers fit_lstm   <- causal_lstm_model(x, lag = 10L, epochs = 30L) fit_retain <- retain_model(x, lag = 10L, epochs = 30L) fit_irnn   <- intervention_rnn_model(x, lag = 10L, epochs = 30L,                                      intervention = interv)

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: tests/test-causalStructureML.R exercises unified causal structure learning API R/causalStructureML.R: causal_structure_ml_model_descriptions(), causalStructureML() method set notears_linear, dag_gnn, notears_nonlinear_mlp, notears_nonlinear_sobolev, grandag, plus data.frame input. Uses devtools::load_all(). Requires expm, igraph, torch (methods except notears_linear); install devtools test runner.

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: tests/test-notears.R runs NOTEARS implementation R/causalDeepNet.R. checks: (1) set_random_seed, (2) is_dag, (3) simulate_dag (ER, SF), (4) simulate_parameter, (5) simulate_linear_sem (gauss, uniform), (6) count_accuracy, (7) notears_linear (L-BFGS-B + augmented Lagrangian, l2 loss), (8) demo_linear, (9) notears_nonlinear NotearsMLP torch available (Adam + L-BFGS, custom autograd trace(expm)). Requires expm igraph; torch optional nonlinear section. Install expm igraph NOTEARS: install.packages(c("expm", "igraph")). nonlinear NOTEARS (MLP/Sobolev), install torch: install.packages("torch").
code: # Full run (includes nonlinear if torch installed) Rscript tests/test-notears.R  # Quick run (skip nonlinear) QUICK=1 Rscript tests/test-notears.R

title: RCausalML: Causal Inference with ML
text: tests/test-causal_structure_learning.R runs causal structure learning algorithms (pure R implementations R/Causal_structure_learning.R): notrears (linear) — NOTEARS linear (Zheng et al.); requires expm uses optim (L-BFGS-B) augmented Lagrangian. notrears (nonlinear) — NOTEARS learnable adjacency (R torch); acyclicity via matrix polynomial. DAG_GNN — DAG structure learning GNN (Yu et al.); VAE + acyclicity (R torch). GraN-DAG — GraN-DAG (Lachapelle et al.); gradient-based neural DAG learning (R torch). notrears tune — Optional Optuna (reticulate) hyperparameter tuning QUICK mode; requires expm NOTEARS linear.
code: # Quick run (small n, d; no tune) QUICK=1 Rscript tests/test-causal_structure_learning.R  # Full run (includes optional Optuna tune) Rscript tests/test-causal_structure_learning.R

Render package root: Notebooks topic (inst/examples/Notebook/): Case studies (inst/examples/Case_study/): customer segmentation individualized incentive response; uplift classification data generation (make_uplift_classification). See inst/examples/Case_study/ files. Render notebooks topic folder (package root), e.g.:

title: BoostedIVForest: Boosted causal forest with instrumental variables — BoostedIVForest
text: Combines CXGBoost cross-fitted nuisance estimation grf::instrumental_forest estimate heterogeneous Local Average Treatment Effects (LATE) presence endogenous treatment.

title: BoostedIVForest: Boosted causal forest with instrumental variables — BoostedIVForest
text: Impute missing values X using per-column medians. Cross-fit nuisances (parallelised, n_folds folds): \(\hat m(X) = E[Y \mid X]\) \(\hat e(X) = E[W \mid X]\) \(\hat p(X) = E[Z \mid X]\) Residualise: \(\tilde Y = Y - \hat m(X)\),     \(\tilde W = W - \hat e(X)\), \(\tilde Z = Z - \hat p(X)\). Fit grf::instrumental_forest     \((\tilde Y, \tilde W, \tilde Z, X)\).

title: BoostedIVForest: Boosted causal forest with instrumental variables — BoostedIVForest
text: Cross-fitting parallelised via future / future.apply. Set parallel plan calling $fit():
code: future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 4)   model$fit(X, Y, W, Z)   future::plan(future::sequential)   # reset afterwards

title: BoostedIVForest: Boosted causal forest with instrumental variables — BoostedIVForest
text: n_folds Number cross-fitting folds. nrounds XGBoost boosting rounds per fold. xgb_params Extra XGBoost hyperparameters. forest_args Extra arguments grf::instrumental_forest. forest Fitted grf::instrumental_forest (post-fit). Y_hat --fold \(E[Y|X]\) (post-fit). W_hat --fold \(E[W|X]\) (post-fit). Z_hat --fold \(E[Z|X]\) (post-fit). iv_diagnostics Weak-instrument diagnostics list (post-fit). imputation_table Per-column medians used NA imputation (post-fit). train_colnames Column names training X (post-fit).

what: DMLearner: Double machine learning (EconML econml.dml.DML family) — DMLearner
title: DMLearner: Double machine learning (EconML econml.dml.DML family) — DMLearner
text: Special case R-learner style two-stage procedure: cross-fit nuisances \(\hat{q}(X) \approx E[Y \mid X]\) \(\hat{f}(X) \approx E[T \mid X]\), form residuals \(\tilde{Y} = Y - \hat{q}(X)\), \(\tilde{T} = T - \hat{f}(X)\), fit CATE model solving (linear parametric case) \(\hat{\theta} = \arg\min E_n[(\tilde{Y} - \Theta(\phi(X)) \cdot \tilde{T})^2]\) .e. OLS cross-product design \(\tilde{T} \otimes \phi(X)\) (EconML cross_product; Python _FinalWrapper._combine). Discrete treatment uses classification-style \(\hat{f}\) (probabilities / residualized binary \(T\)); continuous \(T\) uses regression \(\hat{f}\).

title: DMLearner: Double machine learning (EconML econml.dml.DML family) — DMLearner

what: NOTEARS: Causal DAG structure learning via continuous optimization — NOTEARS
title: NOTEARS: Causal DAG structure learning via continuous optimization — NOTEARS
text: RCausalML package implements NOTEARS (TEARS) causal directed acyclic graph (DAG) structure learning observational data. NOTEARS treats discrete acyclicity constraint continuous constraint \(h(W) = 0\) using trace matrix exponential (\(h(W) = \mathrm{tr}(e^{W \odot W}) - d\)), problem solved continuous optimization (e.g. augmented Lagrangian L-BFGS-B linear, Adam + L-BFGS nonlinear). Linear NOTEARS (pure R): learns weighted adjacency matrix \(W\) minimizing loss (e.g. least squares, logistic, Poisson) plus L1 penalty, subject \(h(W) = 0\). Uses expm L-BFGS-B. Nonlinear NOTEARS (R + torch): uses MLP Sobolev basis models capture nonlinear relationships; acyclicity enforced via differentiable \(h(W)\). Requires torch package. Main functions: notears_linear — Linear NOTEARS: L2/logistic/Poisson loss + L1, augmented Lagrangian. notears_nonlinear — Nonlinear NOTEARS MLP Sobolev model (Adam L-BFGS). NotearsMLP — Torch module: MLP nonlinear SEM (torch installed). NotearsSobolev — Torch module: Sobolev basis nonlinear SEM (torch installed). Utilities simulation: is_dag — Check weighted adjacency matrix DAG. simulate_dag — Simulate random DAG (Erdős–Rényi scale-free). simulate_parameter — Draw edge weights DAG. simulate_linear_sem — Generate data linear SEM (Gaussian, exponential, uniform noise). simulate_nonlinear_sem — Generate data nonlinear SEM (MLP MIM). count_accuracy — Compare estimated graph ground truth (FDR, TPR, FPR, SHD, nnz). set_random_seed — Set R torch seeds reproducibility. Demos: demo_linear — Linear NOTEARS synthetic data. demo_nonlinear_mlp — Nonlinear NOTEARS (MLP) synthetic data. demo_nonlinear_sobolev — Nonlinear NOTEARS (Sobolev) random data.

title: NOTEARS: Causal DAG structure learning via continuous optimization — NOTEARS
text: Zheng, X., Aragam, B., Ravikumar, P., & Xing, E. P. (2018). DAGs TEARS: Continuous optimization structure learning. NeurIPS. https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.01422.

what: Orthogonal Double ML with instrumental variables (OrthoIV) — OrthoIVLearner
title: Orthogonal Double ML with instrumental variables (OrthoIV) — OrthoIVLearner
text: Cross-fitted nuisance regressions IV double ML linear CATE \(\theta(X)^\top \phi(X)\). Solves orthogonal moment (single instrument first column \(Z\) used estimating equations) \(E[(Y - E[Y|X,W] - \theta(X)(T - E[T|X,W]))(Z - E[Z|X,W])] = 0\) projection = FALSE; projection = TRUE, residual treatment uses \(T - E[T|X,W,Z]\) instead \(T - E[T|X,W]\). Python/EconML dependency.

Continuous \(Y\), \(T\), \(Z\) use regression-style fits. binary \(T\) (discrete_treatment = TRUE) binary \(Z\) (discrete_instrument = TRUE 0/1 labels), glmnet xgb use logistic (binomial) objectives; ranger fits forest 0/1 labels; lm linear probability model. what: Precision in Estimation of Heterogeneous Effects (PEHE) — PEHE
title: Precision in Estimation of Heterogeneous Effects (PEHE) — PEHE
text: Root mean squared error true estimated individual treatment effects: \(\sqrt{E[(\tau(x) - \hat\tau(x))^2]}\). Accepts two calling conventions: Matrix form (original): PEHE(y, y_hat)     arguments n x 2 matrices columns [Y(0), Y(1)].     Returns MSE ITE: \(E[((Y(1)-Y(0)) - (\hat Y(1)-\hat Y(0)))^2]\). Vector form (new): PEHE(tau_true = ..., tau_hat = ...)     arguments length-n numeric vectors individual treatment     effects. Returns RMSE: \(\sqrt{E[(\tau - \hat\tau)^2]}\).

title: Precision in Estimation of Heterogeneous Effects (PEHE) — PEHE

title: Precision in Estimation of Heterogeneous Effects (PEHE) — PEHE
text: y n x 2 matrix [Y(0), Y(1)]. Ignored tau_true supplied. y_hat n x 2 matrix [Yhat(0), Yhat(1)]. Ignored tau_hat supplied. tau_true Numeric vector true ITEs (vector form). tau_hat Numeric vector estimated ITEs (vector form).

text: Numeric scalar (RMSE ITE scale). Numeric scalar.

what: Best linear projection of CATE (grf) — best_linear_projection
title: Best linear projection of CATE (grf) — best_linear_projection
text: Re-export best_linear_projection grf package. Estimates best linear projection conditional average treatment effect tau(X) onto user-provided covariates , .e. fits tau(Xi) ~ beta_0 + Ai * beta via regression doubly robust scores forest . case null model equivalent average treatment effect via AIPW. Requires grf package.

title: Best linear projection of CATE (grf) — best_linear_projection
code: best_linear_projection(   forest,   A = NULL,   subset = NULL,   debiasing.weights = NULL,   compliance.score = NULL,   num.trees.for.weights = 500,   vcov.type = "HC3",   target.sample = c("all", "overlap") )

title: Best linear projection of CATE (grf) — best_linear_projection

title: Causal forest with confounders — causal_forest_confounder
text: X covariates used causal regression (tau(X) optionally nuisance). Y outcome (must numeric vector NAs). W treatment assignment (must binary real numeric vector NAs). confounders Optional matrix data frame confounder variables (number rows X). provided, Y.hat W.hat estimated using confounders X (unless Y.hat/W.hat supplied directly). allows residualization control confounding. Default NULL. Y.hat Estimates expected responses E[Y | Xi], marginalizing treatment. Y.hat = NULL, estimated using separate regression forest. confounders provided, regression uses (confounders, X). Default NULL. W.hat Estimates treatment propensities E[W | Xi]. W.hat = NULL, estimated using separate regression forest. confounders provided, regression uses (confounders, X). Default NULL. num.trees Number trees grown forest. Note: Getting accurate confidence intervals generally requires trees getting accurate predictions. Default 2000. sample.weights Weights given sample estimation. NULL, observation receives weight. Note: avoid introducing confounding, weights independent potential outcomes given X. Default NULL. clusters Vector integers factors specifying cluster observation corresponds . Default NULL (ignored). equalize.cluster.weights FALSE, unit given weight (bigger clusters get weight). TRUE, cluster given equal weight forest. case, training, tree uses number observations drawn cluster: smallest cluster K units, sample cluster training, give random K elements cluster tree-growing procedure. estimating average treatment effects, observation given weight 1/cluster size, total weight cluster . Note , argument FALSE, sample weights may also directly adjusted via sample.weights argument. argument TRUE, sample.weights must set NULL. Default FALSE. sample.fraction Fraction data used build tree. Note: honesty = TRUE, subsamples cut factor honesty.fraction. Default 0.5. mtry Number variables tried split. Default \(\sqrt p + 20\) p number variables. min.node.size target minimum number observations tree leaf. Note nodes size smaller min.node.size can occur, original randomForest package. Default 5. honesty Whether use honest splitting (.e., sub-sample splitting). Default TRUE. detailed description honesty, honesty.fraction, honesty.prune.leaves, recommendations parameter tuning, see grf algorithm reference. honesty.fraction fraction data used determining splits honesty = TRUE. Corresponds set J1 notation paper. Default 0.5 (.e. half data used determining splits). honesty.prune.leaves TRUE, prunes estimation sample tree leaves empty. FALSE, keep tree determined splits sample (empty leave encountered, tree skipped contribute estimate). Setting FALSE may improve performance small/marginally powered data, requires trees (note: tuning adjust number trees). applies honesty enabled. Default TRUE. alpha tuning parameter controls maximum imbalance split. Default 0.05. imbalance.penalty tuning parameter controls harshly imbalanced splits penalized. Default 0. stabilize.splits Whether treatment taken account determining imbalance split. Default TRUE. ci.group.size forest grow ci.group.size trees subsample. order provide confidence intervals, ci.group.size must least 2. Default 2. tune.parameters vector parameter names tune. "": tunable parameters tuned cross-validation. following parameters tunable: ("sample.fraction", "mtry", "min.node.size", "honesty.fraction",  "honesty.prune.leaves", "alpha", "imbalance.penalty"). honesty FALSE honesty.* parameters tuned. Default "none" (parameters tuned). tune.num.trees number trees 'mini forest' used fit tuning model. Default 200. tune.num.reps number forests used fit tuning model. Default 50. tune.num.draws number random parameter values considered using model select optimal parameters. Default 1000. compute.oob.predictions Whether OOB predictions training set precomputed. Default TRUE. num.threads Number threads used training. default, number threads set maximum hardware concurrency. seed seed C++ random number generator.

title: Causal forest with confounders — causal_forest_confounder
code: # \donttest{ # Same as causal_forest when confounders = NULL n <- 500 p <- 10 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), n, p) W <- rbinom(n, 1, 0.5) Y <- pmax(X[, 1], 0) * W + X[, 2] + pmin(X[, 3], 0) + rnorm(n) c.forest <- causal_forest_confounder(X, Y, W) #> Error in causal_forest_confounder(X, Y, W): could not find function "causal_forest_confounder"  # With confounders: use separate confounder matrix for nuisance models Z <- matrix(rnorm(n * 3), n, 3)  # 3 confounders c.forest <- causal_forest_confounder(X, Y, W, confounders = Z) #> Error in causal_forest_confounder(X, Y, W, confounders = Z): could not find function "causal_forest_confounder" c.pred <- predict(c.forest, X) #> Error: object 'c.forest' not found # }

Causal Tree (Honest or Adaptive) — causal_tree
text: Fits causal tree heterogeneous treatment effects using recursive partitioning. Supports **adaptive** (original `causalTree` style) **honest** estimation (recommended; Athey & Imbens 2016) one function.

title: Causal Tree (Honest or Adaptive) — causal_tree
code: causal_tree(   formula,   data,   treatment,   honest = TRUE,   est_fraction = 0.5,   est_idx = NULL,   split.Rule = "CT",   cv.option = "CT",   minsize = 5L,   propensity = NULL,   split.alpha = 0.5,   cv.alpha = 0.5,   split.Bucket = FALSE,   bucketNum = 5L,   bucketMax = 100L,   xval = 5L,   cp = 0,   ... )

title: Causal Tree (Honest or Adaptive) — causal_tree
text: formula Formula: response ~ predictors (interactions) data Data frame containing variables `formula` treatment Binary vector (0 = control, 1 = treated), length rows `data` honest Logical. `TRUE` (default), uses honest estimation: tree structure built training sample, leaf means estimated held-estimation sample. est_fraction Fraction data used honest estimation (`honest = TRUE`). Ignored `est_idx` provided. Default: 0.5 est_idx Optional integer vector: row indices use honest estimation. provided, overrides random split `est_fraction`. split.Rule Splitting rule: `"TOT"`, `"CT"`, `"fit"`, `"tstats"` (`"D"` suffix bucketed) cv.option Cross-validation method: `"none"`, `"TOT"`, `"matching"`, `"CT"`, `"fit"`, `"user"`, `"policy"` minsize Minimum number treated **** control units per leaf. Default: 5L propensity Scalar propensity score (constant). Defaults observed mean(treatment) split.alpha Parameter honest splitting risk function (01). Default: 0.5 cv.alpha Parameter CV risk function (01). Default: 0.5 split.Bucket Logical. Use bucketed (discrete) splitting? bucketNum, bucketMax Bucket parameters (`split.Bucket = TRUE`) xval Number cross-validation folds. Default: 5 cp Complexity parameter (can 0 pruning growth) ... Passed `rpart.control()` (e.g. `minsplit`, `maxdepth`, `xval`)

title: Causal Tree (Honest or Adaptive) — causal_tree
text: Object class `c("causalTree", "rpart")`   - Use `rpart.plot::rpart.plot()` visualize   - Use `prune()` prune via cross-validation error   - Use `predict.causalTree()` (previous response) predictions

title: Causal Tree (Honest or Adaptive) — causal_tree
text: Athey, S., & Imbens, G. (2016). Recursive partitioning heterogeneous causal effects. Proceedings National Academy Sciences. Original implementation: <https://github.com/susanathey/causalTree> Maintained fork: **htetree** CRAN

title: Causal Tree (Honest or Adaptive) — causal_tree
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Honest causal tree (recommended)   ct_honest <- causal_tree(     y ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + x4,     data = mydata,     treatment = mydata$trt,     honest = TRUE,     est_fraction = 0.4,     split.Rule = "CT",     cv.option = "CT",     minsize = 10L,     xval = 5   )    # Prune using CV error   cp_best <- ct_honest$cptable[which.min(ct_honest$cptable[,"xerror"]), "CP"]   pruned <- prune(ct_honest, cp = cp_best)    rpart.plot::rpart.plot(pruned, roundint = FALSE)    # Predict CATE on new data   tau_new <- predict(pruned, newdata = testdata, type = "treatment.effect") } # }

CEVAE (Counterfactual Variational Autoencoder) — cevae
text: Generative model: z ~ p(z), x ~ p(x|z), w ~ p(w|z), y ~ p(y|t,z). Twin outcome nets allow imbalanced treatment. Training: ELBO + log q(t|x) + log q(y|t,x). torch available full VAE fitted; else nnet/ranger placeholder.

title: CEVAE (Counterfactual Variational Autoencoder) — cevae

what: Counterfactual Unit Selection: select units to maximize beta*P(complier|X) + gamma*P(always-taker|X) + theta*P(never-taker|X) + delta*P(defier|X). Requires estimates of P(favourable|X,W=0) and P(favourable|X,W=1) (e.g. from outcome model). Complier: favourable iff treated. Always: favourable either way. Never: never favourable. what: DoubleML DiD wrappers (linear / ranger / xgboost nuisances) — doubleml_did_linear
title: DoubleML DiD wrappers (linear / ranger / xgboost nuisances) — doubleml_did_linear
text: Estimates first-difference outcome \(Y = Y_1 - Y_0\) DoubleML. Binary treatment uses DoubleML::DoubleMLIRM score = "ATTE" (default). Multi-arm discrete (two levels, encoded integers) expanded dummies versus smallest level estimated DoubleML::DoubleMLPLR. User-supplied dummy matrices continuous \(D\) use DoubleMLPLR. Defaults follow usual + DML mapping (see Callaway & Sant'Anna 2021; DoubleML documentation).

what: DragonNet — shared representation and three heads (torch) — dragonnet
title: DragonNet — shared representation and three heads (torch) — dragonnet
text: DragonNet uses shared representation three heads: propensity \(\hat{e}(X)\), \(\hat{Y}(0)\), \(\hat{Y}(1)\), optional targeted regularization (learnable \(\varepsilon\)). Architecture follows [causalml dragonnet.py](https://github.com/uber/causalml/blob/master/causalml/inference/tf/dragonnet.py): 3 shared layers (ELU), propensity head (sigmoid), two outcome branches (2 hidden layers , output).

title: DragonNet — shared representation and three heads (torch) — dragonnet
code: dragonnet(   X,   treatment,   y,   neurons = 200L,   reg_l2 = 0.01,   targeted_reg = TRUE,   ratio_tar = 1,   batch_size = 64L,   val_split = 0.2,   adam_epochs = 30L,   adam_lr = 0.001,   sgd_epochs = 100L,   sgd_lr = 1e-05,   sgd_momentum = 0.9,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: DragonNet — shared representation and three heads (torch) — dragonnet
text: X covariate matrix data.frame treatment binary treatment 0/1 y outcome vector neurons units per shared layer (default 200) reg_l2 L2 regularization outcome heads (default 0.01) targeted_reg TRUE (default), use targeted regularization loss ratio_tar weight targeted regularization term (default 1) batch_size batch size training (default 64) val_split fraction validation (default 0.2) adam_epochs epochs Adam phase (default 30) adam_lr Adam learning rate (default 1e-3) sgd_epochs epochs SGD phase (default 100) sgd_lr SGD learning rate (default 1e-5) sgd_momentum SGD momentum (default 0.9) verbose TRUE, print epoch loss ... ignored

Deep Structural Causal Model (DSCM) — dscm
text: Fits Deep Structural Causal Model following tutorial deep_structural _causalML_DSCMs.ipynb. model contains: (1) treatment mechanism \(p(T \mid X)\) (2) outcome mechanism latent abduction \(q(Z \mid X, T, Y)\) potential-outcome heads \(Y(0), Y(1)\).

title: Deep Structural Causal Model (DSCM) — dscm
code: dscm(   X,   treatment,   y,   y0_true = NULL,   y1_true = NULL,   hidden_dim = 256L,   treatment_hidden_dim = 128L,   latent_dim = 16L,   dropout = 0.1,   num_epochs = 300L,   batch_size = 256L,   learning_rate = 2e-04,   weight_decay = 1e-05,   val_frac = 0.15,   kl_warmup_epochs = 80L,   max_beta = 0.25,   lambda_oracle = 0,   patience = 60L,   max_grad_norm = 5,   verbose = TRUE,   device = NULL,   ... )

title: Deep Structural Causal Model (DSCM) — dscm
text: X Covariate matrix data.frame. treatment Binary treatment vector (0/1). y Outcome vector. y0_true Optional true \(Y(0)\) oracle supervision (e.g., IHDP simulations). y1_true Optional true \(Y(1)\) oracle supervision (e.g., IHDP simulations). hidden_dim Hidden size outcome network (default 256). treatment_hidden_dim Hidden size treatment network (default 128). latent_dim Latent noise dimension abduction (default 16). dropout Dropout rate (default 0.1). num_epochs Number epochs (default 300). batch_size Batch size (default 256). learning_rate AdamW learning rate (default 2e-4). weight_decay AdamW weight decay (default 1e-5). val_frac Validation fraction (default 0.15). kl_warmup_epochs Number KL warmup epochs (default 80). max_beta Maximum KL weight warmup (default 0.25). lambda_oracle Oracle supervision weight (default 0). patience Early stopping patience validation objective (default 60). max_grad_norm Gradient clipping norm (default 5). verbose TRUE, print progress every 25 epochs. device Device string: "cuda", "cpu", NULL. ... Ignored.

text: Uses kernelshap (permshap) compute SHAP values model's CATE predictions, can pass result shapviz() use shapviz functions: sv_importance(), sv_dependence(), sv_waterfall(), sv_force(), sv_dependence2D(), etc.

title: Compute SHAP values for any RCausalML CATE model — explain_cate
code: explain_cate(   object,   X,   bg_X = NULL,   n_samples = NULL,   use_permshap = FALSE,   pred_fun = NULL,   ... )

title: Compute SHAP values for any RCausalML CATE model — explain_cate
text: object Fitted RCausalML model (). X Feature matrix data.frame explain (covariates used fitting). bg_X Optional background dataset kernelshap; NULL, small sample X may used kernelshap (see ?kernelshap::kernelshap). n_samples Optional number rows explain (speed). NULL, rows X used. use_permshap TRUE, use kernelshap::permshap() instead kernelshap::kernelshap() (faster, approximate). Recommended ncol(X) > 8. pred_fun Optional custom prediction function form function(object, newdata) returning numeric vector. NULL, default wrapper around predict(object, newdata) used. ... Passed kernelshap::kernelshap() kernelshap::permshap() (e.g. n_permutations, verbose).

title: Compute SHAP values for any RCausalML CATE model — explain_cate
text: object class kernelshap. Pass shapviz() get shapviz object, use sv_importance(), sv_dependence(), sv_waterfall(), sv_force(), etc.

title: Compute SHAP values for any RCausalML CATE model — explain_cate
text: Works RCausalML models predict(object, newdata) returning CATE (list predictions), including: Meta-learners: SLearner, TLearner, XLearner, RLearner, DRLearner DML: LinearDML, SparseLinearDML, KernelDML, NonParamDML, CausalForestDML, DynamicDMLearner, OrthoIVLearner, DMLIVLearner, NonParamDMLIVLearner Trees/forests: causal_forest, uplift_rf_*, uplift_tree_*, interaction_tree, causal_inference_tree Policy: policy_learner, DRPolicyTree, DRPolicyForest Interpreters: SingleTreeCateInterpreter, SingleTreePolicyInterpreter Neural: cevae, dragonnet, tarnet, cfrnet, ganite : AutomatedMLModel, driv_learner

title: Compute SHAP values for any RCausalML CATE model — explain_cate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(RCausalML) library(kernelshap) library(shapviz)  # Fit any CATE model set.seed(42) d <- synthetic_data(mode = 1, n = 500, p = 5, sigma = 1) sl <- SLearner(learner = "ranger") sl <- fit(sl, d$X, d$w, d$y)  # Compute SHAP for CATE predictions X_explain <- d$X[1:100, ]   # optional: subset for speed ks <- explain_cate(sl, X_explain, bg_X = d$X, use_permshap = TRUE)  # Build shapviz object and use all shapviz functions shp <- shapviz(ks) sv_importance(shp) sv_dependence(shp, "X1") sv_waterfall(shp, 1) sv_force(shp, 1) } # }

Instrumental forest (grf) — instrumental_forest
text: Re-export instrumental_forest grf package. Trains instrumental forest conditional local average treatment effects using instruments. Formally, forest estimates tau(X) = Cov[Y, Z | X = x] / Cov[W, Z | X = x]. Z W coincide, instrumental forest equivalent causal forest. Requires grf package.

title: Load a causaldata dataset in RCausalML-ready form — load_causaldata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(RCausalML) # List supported datasets list_causaldata_datasets()  # NSW experimental data (Mixtape) d <- load_causaldata("nsw_mixtape") sl <- SLearner(learner = "ranger") sl <- fit(sl, d$X, d$w, d$y) estimate_ate(sl, d$X, d$w, d$y) # Cite the data citation("causaldata") print(d$citation)  # IV: college proximity and wages (Card) d_iv <- load_causaldata("close_college") # Use d_iv$X, d_iv$w (educ), d_iv$y (lwage), d_iv$Z (nearc4) with OrthoIVLearner } # }

what: Policy Learner: treatment assignment policy with doubly robust estimator — policy_learner
title: Policy Learner: treatment assignment policy with doubly robust estimator — policy_learner
text: Learns treatment assignment policy observational data using doubly robust estimator causal effect binary treatment. policy trained maximize expected outcome (welfare) fitting classifier sign DR score weights equal absolute DR score. Aligns "weighted classification" approach Athey & Wager (2018); tree/forest policy learners see DRPolicyTree DRPolicyForest.

title: Policy Learner: treatment assignment policy with doubly robust estimator — policy_learner

title: Policy Learner: treatment assignment policy with doubly robust estimator — policy_learner

text: Cross-fits nuisance regressions \(E[Y \mid D, X]\) \(E[D \mid X]\), runs weighted local linear regressions residuals dose_grid (Epanechnikov weights). Base learners match RLearner: "ranger", "lm", "xgb".

title: Generalized R-learner for continuous treatment (dose-response on a grid) — r_learner_continuous
code: r_learner_continuous(   data,   outcome = "Y",   treatment = "D",   covariates = NULL,   dose_grid = seq(0, 6, by = 0.5),   learner = c("ranger", "lm", "xgb"),   n_fold = 5L,   bandwidth = NULL,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Generalized R-learner for continuous treatment (dose-response on a grid) — r_learner_continuous
text: data data.frame outcome, treatment, covariates. outcome Name outcome column (default "Y"). treatment Name continuous treatment column (default "D"). covariates Character vector covariate column names. NULL, uses paste0("X", 1:10) columns exist, otherwise columns except outcome treatment. dose_grid Dose values estimate \(\partial E[Y(d)\mid X]/\partial d\) (local linear slope \(D\)). learner "ranger", "lm", "xgb" (spirit RLearner). n_fold Number cross-fitting folds nuisance models. bandwidth Kernel half-width local regression; default sd(D) * n^(-1/5). seed Optional integer; set, fold assignment reproducible. ... Passed ranger::ranger xgboost::xgboost.

title: Generalized R-learner for continuous treatment (dose-response on a grid) — r_learner_continuous

what: Total loss for the Temporal Causal VAE — temporal_causal_loss
title: Total loss for the Temporal Causal VAE — temporal_causal_loss
text: Combines four terms:   1. Reconstruction loss — Gaussian log-likelihood *last* timestep   2. KL loss             — KL( q(z|x) || N(0,1) ), analytical   3. DAG loss            — NOTEARS acyclicity penalty   4. Sparsity loss       — L1 adjacency matrix entries

title: Total loss for the Temporal Causal VAE — temporal_causal_loss
code: temporal_causal_loss(x, dec_mu, dec_logvar, enc_mu, enc_logvar, model)

title: Total loss for the Temporal Causal VAE — temporal_causal_loss
text: x Ground-truth sequence  [batch, T, obs_dim] dec_mu Decoder mean           [batch, obs_dim] dec_logvar Decoder log-variance   [batch, obs_dim] enc_mu Encoder mean           [batch, latent_dim] enc_logvar Encoder log-variance   [batch, latent_dim] model TemporalCausalVAE instance (hyperparams + penalty methods)

title: Total loss for the Temporal Causal VAE — temporal_causal_loss
text: Scalar tensor — total loss (lower better)

title: Total loss for the Temporal Causal VAE — temporal_causal_loss
text: total = -recon + beta * KL + gamma * (DAG + lambda_sparsity * sparsity)

title: Total loss for the Temporal Causal VAE — temporal_causal_loss

what: Test calibration of a grf forest — test_calibration
title: Test calibration of a grf forest — test_calibration
text: Re-export test_calibration grf package. Omnibus evaluation quality random forest estimates via calibration. Computes best linear fit target estimand using forest prediction (held-data) mean forest prediction regressors. coefficient 1 mean.forest.prediction suggests mean forest prediction correct; coefficient 1 differential.forest.prediction suggests heterogeneity estimates well calibrated. p-value differential.forest.prediction also acts omnibus test presence heterogeneity. Requires grf package.

title: Test calibration of a grf forest — test_calibration

RCausalML 0.3.0
text: DAG-GNN exports (R/causalDeepNet.R): make_daggnn, daggnn_adj, DAGGNN, get_daggnn_device, preprocess_adj, matrix_poly exported RCausalML (bundled R/causalDeepNet.R alongside NOTEARS). NOTEARS exports (R/causalDeepNet.R): NOTEARS structure-learning helpers (notears_linear, notears_nonlinear, NotearsMLP, NotearsSobolev, simulate_dag, count_accuracy, related utilities) now part installed RCausalML namespace (formerly standalone R/notears.R). uplift_randomForest() (R/uplift_randomForest.R): unified uplift random forest API aligned Python UpliftRandomForestClassifier heterogeneous treatment effects. Supports classification (bagged KL / ED / Chi / CTS / DDP / IDDP trees) regression (T-learner ranger forests bagged / CIT trees). Multi-arm experiments fit one forest per non-control arm. Wrappers: uplift_randomForestClassifier(), uplift_randomForestRegressor(). Test script: tests/test-uplift_randomForest.R. uplift_xgboost() (R/uplift_xgboost.R): T-learner XGBoost uplift model API shape uplift_randomForest(). Fits separate XGBoost outcome models per treatment arm estimates CATE predicted outcome difference. Supports binary multi-arm treatment, classification regression outcomes, full_output predictions. Wrappers: uplift_xgboostClassifier(), uplift_xgboostRegressor(). Requires optional xgboost package. Test script: tests/test-uplift_xgboost.R.

title: RCausalML 0.3.0
text: predict.DMLearner — NA coefficient propagation (R/DMLearner.R): stats::lm() drops aliased/collinear columns final OLS stage (LinearDML, KernelDML), sets coefficients NA. manual coef %*% phi(X) multiply now replaces NA coefficients 0 (treating dropped directions zero contribution) matrix multiplication, preventing -NA CATE predictions. Applied "lm" "kernel" branches predict.DMLearner. predict.DMLearner — finite-value guarantee (R/DMLearner.R): final safety guard end predict.DMLearner replaces residual NA / NaN / Inf returned prediction vector mean finite values (0 everything non-finite). ensures downstream callers kernelshap::permshap shapviz never receive non-finite values, fixing “missing SHAP values allowed” error computing explain_cate() DML models. rcausalml_predict_numeric — SHAP pred_fun guard (R/shapviz_integration.R): prediction wrapper used explain_cate() now replaces NA / NaN / Inf output mean finite values returning, providing belt--suspenders guarantee permshap / kernelshap never receives non-finite predictions regardless underlying model type. lst-nmes-dml-fit-compare Spearman guard (tutorials/02-03-meta-learners-continuous-treatment-r.qmd): Added complete.cases(M) >= 3 check Spearman correlation loop degenerate-model edge case emits warning leaves matrix NA rather crashing “complete element pairs”.

title: RCausalML 0.3.0
text: configure.win: Added Windows-specific configure script (POSIX sh via Rtools) mirrors Unix configure script. interactive installs reports missing/outdatable R package dependencies installs using type = "binary" (preferred Windows avoid source compilation). Post-install guidance torch::install_torch() reticulate::install_miniconda() printed automatically packages target set. Ubuntu prerequisites: help file (HELP_RCausalML_0.3.0.md) now documents full set system-level apt packages required installing R dependencies native code (libssl-dev, libcurl4-openssl-dev, libxml2-dev, LAPACK/BLAS, freetype/harfbuzz, python3-dev, cmake, etc.) includes CRAN PPA recipe current R build. Windows prerequisites: help file now documents Rtools installation (Rtools44 R 4.4.x, Rtools43 R 4.3.x, etc.), PATH configuration, binary-package preference (type = "binary"), VC++ Redistributable requirements torch, Miniconda setup reticulate, long-path registry fix. Expanded troubleshooting: Section 34 help file now split Cross-platform, Ubuntu-specific, Windows-specific sub-tables covering common install failures platform.

title: RCausalML 0.3.0
text: DESCRIPTION: version 0.3.0 (2026-05-21). HELP_RCausalML_0.3.0.md: new help file replacing 0.1.8 version; tarball-install code snippets updated reference RCausalML_0.3.0.tar.gz.

what: New models — Structural Causal Models (SCMs) with Deep Components (R/causalDeepNet.R)
title: RCausalML 0.1.9
text: Three new model families converted Python (Pearl’s SCM hierarchy — association, intervention, counterfactual) two graph-utility functions: DeepSCM (deep_scm()): Fixed-graph Structural Causal Model. variable’s structural equation small MLP (StructuralEquationNet) conditioned graph-parents per-variable latent noise vector inferred variational encoder (NoiseEncoder). Training minimises ELBO-style objective (MSE reconstruction + KL). Supports predict.deep_scm() intervene_deep_scm() -calculus interventions (returns predictions low/high values mean delta). Adjacency matrix can supplied derived correlation heuristic. CamelCase alias: deepSCM(). DECI (deci_model()): Deep End--end Causal Inference. Jointly learns soft causal adjacency matrix (DECIAdjacency) nonlinear structural equations (DECIEncoder + DECIDecoder) lagged time-series windows. Uses NOTEARS acyclicity penalty  plus KL L1 sparsity ELBO objective. Returns A_soft A_binary (thresholded). Supports predict.deci_model() ate_deci() Monte-Carlo average treatment effect estimation. CamelCase alias: deciModel(). DynoTEARS (dynotears()): Lagged causal discovery multivariate time series. Learns lag-specific weight matrices  augmented-Lagrangian DAG constraint , L1 sparsity, progressive rho/alpha updates. Returns A_binary, W_agg, training diagnostics (train_losses, dag_vals). Supports predict.dynotears(). CamelCase alias: dynoTEARS(). evaluate_graph_recovery(A_true, A_pred, name): Computes TP, FP, FN, Precision, Recall, F1, Structural Hamming Distance (SHD) predicted binary adjacency matrix ground-truth DAG. plot_scm_dag(, var_names, title): Visualises weighted binary adjacency matrix heatmap (ggplot2 + reshape2 available, base-graphics fallback). Diagonal entries masked.

title: RCausalML 0.1.9
text: DESCRIPTION: version 0.1.9 (2026-05-19); extended description cover DeepSCM, DECI, DynoTEARS, evaluate_graph_recovery, plot_scm_dag; added reshape2 Suggests. NAMESPACE: exported deep_scm, predict.deep_scm, intervene_deep_scm, deci_model, predict.deci_model, ate_deci, dynotears, predict.dynotears, evaluate_graph_recovery, plot_scm_dag, deepSCM, deciModel, dynoTEARS; registered S3 methods predict.deep_scm, predict.deci_model, predict.dynotears. README: updated intro, algorithms table (new SCM Deep Components row), dependencies section, neural-test description.

title: RCausalML 0.1.9
text: Causal XGBoost (R/causalXGBoost.R): CXGBoost R6 class implementing two-head DragonNet-style masked-MSE causal outcome model XGBoost ranger propensity scorer. New methods: fit(), predict(), summary(), evaluate() (PEHE + ATE vs ground-truth), plot_importance() (ggplot2 variable-importance chart), save_model() / load_model(), clone_reset(). Exported evaluation metrics PEHE() ATE() accept matrix vector calling conventions. Bug fixes vs v1.0: Hessian always > 0, ranger column selection .max, verbose fully respected parallel workers, XGBoost label flattening. Exported: CXGBoost, PEHE, ATE, ATE_error, run_example. Multi-arm Causal Boosting (R/multi_arm_causal_boost.R): MultiArmCausalBoost R6 class extending binary CXGBoost K ≥ 2 treatment arms. Fits separate XGBoost outcome models per arm multiclass ranger propensity forest. Supports univariate multivariate Y, factor treatment, NA median imputation, treatment contrast reports relative chosen baseline arm. Methods: fit(), predict(), predict_ate(), summary(), plot_importance(), save() / load(). Exported: MultiArmCausalBoost, multi_arm_PEHE, multi_arm_ATE, load_multi_arm_causal_boost, save_multi_arm_causal_boost, run_multi_arm_causal_boost_example. Interventional Causal Representation Learning (R/interventionalCRL.R): InterventionalCRL torch nn_module — environment-conditioned VAE two-hidden-layer encoder/decoder analytically tractable KL divergence q(z|x,e) environment prior p(z|e). Designed binary Morgan-fingerprint inputs (Bernoulli BCE reconstruction). interventional_elbo_loss() computes negative ELBO (BCE + KL) scalar tensor. Optional device auto-selection (CUDA / CPU). Exported via @export tempoCausalVAE.R (TemporalCausalVAE, temporal_causal_loss). Temporal Causal VAE (R/tempoCausalVAE.R): TemporalCausalVAE torch nn_module — GRU encoder/decoder learned latent_dim × latent_dim causal adjacency matrix, NOTEARS-style DAG acyclicity penalty, L1 sparsity adjacency. temporal_causal_loss() computes combined ELBO + DAG + sparsity loss. Beta-VAE KL weighting (beta) separate adjacency penalty weight (gamma) configurable. Exported: TemporalCausalVAE, temporal_causal_loss. Temporal Causal Discovery Framework (R/temporalCausaDiscovery.R): R/torch port TCDF (Nauta et al., https://github.com/M-Nauta/TCDF). Implements depthwise causal TCN building blocks (TCDF_chomp1d, TCDF_first_block, TCDF_temporal_block, TCDF_last_block, TCDF_depthwise_net) TCDF_ADDSTCN attention-based dilated TCN channel-wise causal discovery multivariate time series. Requires torch.

title: RCausalML 0.1.9
text: cevae(): Full CEVAE implementation torch installed, aligned Python CausalML/Pyro: generative model (z p(z)), (x p(x|z)), (w p(w|z)), (y p(y|t,z)) twin outcome nets imbalanced treatment. Training uses ELBO plus (q(t|x)) (q(y|t,x)) (TraceCausalEffect_ELBO style). New arguments: outcome_dist, latent_dim, hidden_dim, num_epochs, num_layers, batch_size, learning_rate, learning_rate_decay, num_samples, weight_decay, verbose. Falls back nnet ranger placeholder torch available. predict.cevae(): torch CEVAE, ITE via Monte Carlo samples guide model; optional num_samples predict. fit_predict_cevae(): New convenience function fit--predict one call (matches Python CEVAE.fit_predict(X, treatment, y)).

title: RCausalML 0.1.9
text: dragonnet(): Full DragonNet implementation torch package installed: shared representation (3 ELU layers), propensity head, two outcome heads ((0)) ((1)), learnable epsilon targeted regularization. Training follows CausalML (Adam SGD momentum). New arguments: neurons, reg_l2, targeted_reg, ratio_tar, batch_size, val_split, adam_epochs/sgd_epochs, verbose. Falls back nnet ranger placeholder torch available. predict.dragonnet(): Supports torch models; new option propensity = TRUE return estimated propensity scores (torch ).
